assets.name: lecture_09_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 01 is the clearest and least occluded frame. It preserves both the left-hand component notation for the cross product and the right-hand three-by-three antisymmetric-matrix sketch, while later frames place more of Susskind's body in front of the board and crop the left expression more aggressively.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: And how many components does an anti-symmetric matrix have?

assets.name: lecture_09_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 04 gives the cleanest usable board view in this window. Both equations are fully legible, the board layout is preserved, and Susskind has moved far enough to the right that the mathematical content is less visually dominated by his body than in the earlier frames.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: before we get into Lagrangians and Hamiltonians

assets.name: lecture_09_figure_05.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 04 shows the most finished version of the equation in this subtitle window. The left-hand action symbol, the integral sign, the developing integrand, and the trailing \(dt\) are all visible together, whereas the earlier frames are mostly just the empty setup before the expression is written in earnest.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: The action is the integral of a Lagrangian,
assets.caption_hint: action as an integral of the Lagrangian
